groups.title: Essentials
groups.description: Minimum topic, goal, audience, message, factual detail, format, language, length, and CTA context for an accurate Telegram post.

groups.title: Message & Voice
groups.description: Tone, channel voice, emoji level, and Telegram-compatible formatting preferences.

groups.title: Links & Publishing
groups.description: Destination URL, button label, timing, and hashtag preferences for Telegram publishing workflows.

groups.title: Accuracy & Output
groups.description: Evidence, disclosures, restrictions, variant count, and additional constraints.

variables.label: Topic, Product, or Offer
variables.placeholder: What should the Telegram post be about?
variables.hint: Enter the main topic, announcement, product, event, or offer.

help.what: Provide the topic, product, or offer for this Telegram post.
help.why: It establishes the central subject of the post.
help.example: Good example: Creatornivo Early Access update
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, multiple competing topics, invented context, or claims the post cannot support.

variables.label: Primary Goal
variables.placeholder: Select the main purpose
variables.hint: Choose the single most important outcome for this post.

help.what: Provide the primary goal for this Telegram post.
help.why: The goal determines the post structure, emphasis, and CTA.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, multiple competing topics, invented context, or claims the post cannot support.

variables.label: Target Audience
variables.placeholder: Who should read this post, and what matters to them?
variables.hint: Describe the intended readers and any relevant interests or needs.

help.what: Provide the target audience for this Telegram post.
help.why: Audience context materially changes vocabulary, examples, framing, and detail.
help.example: Good example: Existing Creatornivo users and creators following product updates.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, multiple competing topics, invented context, or claims the post cannot support.

variables.label: Core Message
variables.placeholder: What should readers understand or remember?
variables.hint: State the main takeaway in plain language.

help.what: Provide the core message for this Telegram post.
help.why: It prevents the generated post from drifting away from the intended takeaway.
help.example: Good example: Password reset and sign-out polish are live, and generation limits are clearer.
help.avoid: Avoid vague placeholders, multiple competing topics, invented context, or claims the post cannot support.

variables.label: Facts and Required Details
variables.placeholder: Names, dates, prices, conditions, features, or required points
variables.hint: Include only confirmed information that must appear in the post.

help.what: Provide the facts and required details for this Telegram post.
help.why: It supplies the factual information the model must not infer or fabricate.
help.example: Good example: Free plan has 5 generations per UTC day. Pro has 100 generations per UTC month. Support email is support@creatornivo.com.
help.avoid: Avoid assumptions, fake dates, invented prices, fabricated results, unsupported links, or vague filler.

variables.label: Post Format
variables.placeholder: Choose how the post will be published
variables.hint: Media captions require a much shorter result than standalone posts.

help.what: Optional context for the post format field.
help.why: Text posts and media captions require different length handling.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Output Language
variables.placeholder: English, Ukrainian, Spanish, or another language
variables.hint: The model will write the complete output in this language.

help.what: Optional context for the output language field.
help.why: Language cannot always be safely inferred from names or source material.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Channel Context
variables.placeholder: What does the channel publish, and what do readers expect?
variables.hint: Add the channel topic, relationship with readers, or publishing context.

help.what: Optional context for the channel context field.
help.why: The same announcement may require different framing in a news, business, educational, or personal channel.
help.example: Good example: Product update channel for an early-access AI content workflow product.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Length
variables.placeholder: Choose a preferred length
variables.hint: Auto selects a practical length while respecting the selected post format.

help.what: Optional context for the length field.
help.why: Users may need anything from a short update to a detailed channel post.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Reader Action
variables.placeholder: What should readers do next?
variables.hint: Choose one primary action or let the model select it.

help.what: Optional context for the reader action field.
help.why: It aligns the ending with the intended user action without creating competing CTAs.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Tone
variables.placeholder: Select a tone
variables.hint: Choose the overall communication style.

help.what: Optional context for the tone field.
help.why: Tone materially changes wording and audience perception.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Brand Voice
variables.placeholder: Describe the usual voice or paste a short style example
variables.hint: Add distinctive voice rules only when the default tone is insufficient.

help.what: Optional context for the brand voice field.
help.why: It helps the result remain consistent with an established channel identity.
help.example: Good example: Practical, transparent, and product-focused.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Emoji Use
variables.placeholder: Choose an emoji level
variables.hint: Controls whether emojis support scanning or are omitted.

help.what: Optional context for the emoji use field.
help.why: Emoji expectations vary substantially between Telegram channels.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Telegram Formatting
variables.placeholder: Choose a publishing format
variables.hint: Select plain copy, manual formatting, or bot-compatible markup.

help.what: Optional context for the telegram formatting field.
help.why: Telegram HTML, MarkdownV2, and manually formatted posts require different output syntax.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Destination URL
variables.placeholder: https://www.creatornivo.com/updates
variables.hint: Add the exact page readers should open.

help.what: Optional context for the destination url field.
help.why: The model must not invent or modify destination links.
help.avoid: Avoid invented, shortened, tracking-modified, or unsupported links.

variables.label: Button Text
variables.placeholder: Read More, Join Now, View Details
variables.hint: Leave blank to generate a concise label automatically.

help.what: Optional context for the button text field.
help.why: It allows control over the optional Telegram inline-button label.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Dates and Timing
variables.placeholder: Launch date, deadline, event time, timezone, or availability
variables.hint: Include confirmed time-sensitive information that affects the message.

help.what: Optional context for the dates and timing field.
help.why: Announcements and events often depend on exact dates, deadlines, and timezones.
help.example: Good example: Published after the July 2026 maintenance release.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Hashtags
variables.placeholder: Choose a hashtag approach
variables.hint: Telegram posts usually need few or no hashtags.

help.what: Optional context for the hashtags field.
help.why: Some channels use hashtags for navigation, while others avoid them entirely.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Evidence and Sources
variables.placeholder: Paste approved statistics, quotations, source notes, or URLs
variables.hint: Supply evidence for claims that should not rely only on general wording.

help.what: Optional context for the evidence and sources field.
help.why: It allows factual claims to be grounded without asking users for a separate research workflow.
help.example: Good example: Use only the supplied release notes and public product limits.
help.avoid: Avoid unsupported statistics, quotes, studies, proof, or claims that the supplied source does not establish.

variables.label: Disclosures and Restrictions
variables.placeholder: Sponsorship disclosure, prohibited claims, privacy rules, or topics to avoid
variables.hint: Add mandatory disclosures and anything the post must not say.

help.what: Optional context for the disclosures and restrictions field.
help.why: Commercial, regulated, or sensitive posts may require explicit boundaries.
help.example: Good example: Do not imply customer numbers, ratings, revenue, urgency, or scarcity.
help.avoid: Avoid hiding commercial relationships, privacy boundaries, regulated-topic limits, or prohibited claims.

variables.label: Number of Variants
variables.placeholder: Choose the number of versions
variables.hint: Additional versions use different angles without changing the supplied facts.

help.what: Optional context for the number of variants field.
help.why: Some users need alternatives, but excessive variations would make a compact template inefficient.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.

variables.label: Additional Context
variables.placeholder: Add any useful instruction not covered above
variables.hint: Use this only for information that materially affects the result.

help.what: Optional context for the additional context field.
help.why: It provides one controlled place for uncommon requirements without multiplying form fields.
help.example: Good example: Keep the post calm and easy to scan on mobile.
help.avoid: Avoid guessed facts, fake urgency, fake scarcity, engagement bait, unsupported social proof, or extra CTAs.
